question:
Marine operator Choi is a veteran who has worked on this river for 15 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 347km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 17 units of construction material (61kg each) and 10 units of relief supply box (30kg each). The boat can travel at 35km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 27km is Zone A (speed limit: 29km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 39%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 39%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 140km waypoint, 4 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 117 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 81 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 37 minutes (1st: 81min, 2nd: 118min, 3rd: 155min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Cargo: 61kg construction materialx17 + 30kg relief supply boxx10
  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -39%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 140km, relief supply box x4
  - Cumulative fatigue: +37min per rest

question:
River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 339km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 21 units of relief supply box (55kg each) and 6 units of construction material (80kg each). The boat can travel at 25km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 36km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 275km waypoint, 8 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 115 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 73 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 31 minutes (1st: 73min, 2nd: 104min, 3rd: 135min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
River courier Seo is a veteran who has worked on this river for 14 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 326km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 16 units of relief supply box (47kg each) and 5 units of medical kit (7kg each). The boat can travel at 30km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 32km is Zone A (speed limit: 26km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 40%.

From 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 38%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 264km waypoint, 5 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 113 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 57 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 27 minutes (1st: 57min, 2nd: 84min, 3rd: 111min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 317km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 11 units of construction material (70kg each) and 7 units of medical kit (15kg each). The boat can travel at 30km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 5 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 309km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 10 units of construction material (80kg each) and 7 units of medical kit (10kg each). The boat can travel at 33km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 22km is Zone A (speed limit: 27km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 39%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 30km waypoint, 3 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 100 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 58 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 36 minutes (1st: 58min, 2nd: 94min, 3rd: 130min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Cargo: 80kg construction materialx10 + 10kg medical kitx7
  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~2:00 PM, Zone B -39%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 30km, medical kit x3
  - Cumulative fatigue: +36min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 22, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 27, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 15}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 100, 'duration_minutes': 58, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 200, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 22, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 34}}

question:
Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 13 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 326km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 16 units of medical kit (11kg each) and 8 units of construction material (66kg each). The boat can travel at 24km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 41km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 243km waypoint, 4 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 114 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 72 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 29 minutes (1st: 72min, 2nd: 101min, 3rd: 130min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Cargo: 11kg medical kitx16 + 66kg construction materialx8
  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~2:00 PM, Zone B -33%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 243km, construction material x4
  - Cumulative fatigue: +29min per rest

question:
River courier Seo is a veteran who has worked on this river for 15 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 349km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 12 units of relief supply box (49kg each) and 6 units of water barrel (24kg each). The boat can travel at 32km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 23km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 24% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 32%.

From 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 60km waypoint, 4 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 96 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 78 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 78min, 2nd: 108min, 3rd: 138min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 49kg relief supply boxx12 + 24kg water barrelx6
  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~3:00 PM, Zone B -33%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 60km, relief supply box x4
  - Cumulative fatigue: +30min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 31, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 23, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 14}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 96, 'duration_minutes': 78, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 24, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 32}}

question:
Riverside logistics driver Yoon is a veteran who has worked on this river for 14 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 322km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 17 units of medical kit (8kg each) and 10 units of water barrel (23kg each). The boat can travel at 29km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 43km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 25% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 32%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 230km waypoint, 6 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 98 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 84 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 84min, 2nd: 114min, 3rd: 144min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 7km/h (downstream in Zone A)
  - Cargo: 8kg medical kitx17 + 23kg water barrelx10
  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -32%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 230km, water barrel x6
  - Cumulative fatigue: +30min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 43, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 25, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 16}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 98, 'duration_minutes': 84, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 200, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 25, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 36}}

question:
Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 343km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 21 units of medical kit (12kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (44kg each). The boat can travel at 22km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 33km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 25% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 37%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 32%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 290km waypoint, 10 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 119 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 69 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 32 minutes (1st: 69min, 2nd: 101min, 3rd: 133min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Cargo: 12kg medical kitx21 + 44kg relief supply boxx5
  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -32%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 290km, medical kit x10
  - Cumulative fatigue: +32min per rest

question:
River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 11 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 310km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 22 units of construction material (72kg each) and 9 units of relief supply box (59kg each). The boat can travel at 31km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 13km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 17% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 110km waypoint, 8 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 111 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 83 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 83min, 2nd: 113min, 3rd: 143min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~5:00 PM, Zone B -34%

  - Cumulative fatigue: +30min per rest

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 15 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 315km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 10 units of construction material (77kg each) and 9 units of water barrel (18kg each). The boat can travel at 24km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 38km is Zone A (speed limit: 17km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 17% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 32%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 66km waypoint, 3 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 119 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 52 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 34 minutes (1st: 52min, 2nd: 86min, 3rd: 120min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~5:00 PM, Zone B -32%

  - Cumulative fatigue: +34min per rest

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 315km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 22 units of medical kit (14kg each) and 5 units of water barrel (17kg each). The boat can travel at 27km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 42km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 230km waypoint, 3 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 118 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 49 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 33 minutes (1st: 49min, 2nd: 82min, 3rd: 115min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Cumulative fatigue: +33min per rest

question:
Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 5 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 317km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 22 units of medical kit (11kg each) and 7 units of relief supply box (58kg each). The boat can travel at 30km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 26km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 88km waypoint, 3 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 95 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 61 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 35 minutes (1st: 61min, 2nd: 96min, 3rd: 131min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Riverside logistics driver Yoon is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 350km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 20 units of medical kit (14kg each) and 4 units of construction material (70kg each). The boat can travel at 24km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 32%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 120km waypoint, 8 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 99 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 52 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 35 minutes (1st: 52min, 2nd: 87min, 3rd: 122min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

question:
Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 359km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 16 units of relief supply box (43kg each) and 4 units of medical kit (12kg each). The boat can travel at 27km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 44km is Zone A (speed limit: 20km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 13km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 25% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 66km waypoint, 8 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 120 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 65 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 27 minutes (1st: 65min, 2nd: 92min, 3rd: 119min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 320km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 10 units of relief supply box (69kg each) and 8 units of water barrel (18kg each). The boat can travel at 33km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 44km is Zone A (speed limit: 26km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 24% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 40%.

From 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 36%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 121km waypoint, 5 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 119 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 52 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 32 minutes (1st: 52min, 2nd: 84min, 3rd: 116min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:
STEP0=meta · STEP1=given · STEP2=worked solution · STEP3=answer and verification
[STEP 0] Problem meta

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 11 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 334km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 11 units of construction material (74kg each) and 8 units of relief supply box (70kg each). The boat can travel at 26km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 34km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 36%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 150km waypoint, 5 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 111 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 75 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 75min, 2nd: 105min, 3rd: 135min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Inland navigator Jung is a veteran who has worked on this river for 13 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 357km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 11 units of construction material (54kg each) and 5 units of medical kit (7kg each). The boat can travel at 32km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 27km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 15% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 39%.

From 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 39%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 110km waypoint, 7 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 107 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 75 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 28 minutes (1st: 75min, 2nd: 103min, 3rd: 131min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Cargo: 54kg construction materialx11 + 7kg medical kitx5
  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~5:00 PM, Zone B -39%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 110km, construction material x7
  - Cumulative fatigue: +28min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 31, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 27, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 16}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 107, 'duration_minutes': 75, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 200, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 15, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 39}}

question:
Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 334km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 20 units of water barrel (22kg each) and 10 units of medical kit (13kg each). The boat can travel at 34km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 32km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 39%.

From 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 150km waypoint, 7 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 118 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 46 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 29 minutes (1st: 46min, 2nd: 75min, 3rd: 104min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Cargo: 22kg water barrelx20 + 13kg medical kitx10
  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~1:00 PM, Zone B -34%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 150km, water barrel x7
  - Cumulative fatigue: +29min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 32, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 25, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 15}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 118, 'duration_minutes': 46, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 22, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 39}}

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 13 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 351km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 14 units of relief supply box (69kg each) and 8 units of medical kit (8kg each). The boat can travel at 27km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 25km is Zone A (speed limit: 23km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 19% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 90km waypoint, 6 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 95 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 67 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 34 minutes (1st: 67min, 2nd: 101min, 3rd: 135min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 7km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 69kg relief supply boxx14 + 8kg medical kitx8
  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -33%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 90km, relief supply box x6
  - Cumulative fatigue: +34min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 25, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 23, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 16}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 95, 'duration_minutes': 67, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 19, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 33}}

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 352km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 16 units of relief supply box (41kg each) and 4 units of water barrel (15kg each). The boat can travel at 29km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 35km is Zone A (speed limit: 26km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 25% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 39%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 330km waypoint, 2 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 118 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 47 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 47min, 2nd: 77min, 3rd: 107min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 41kg relief supply boxx16 + 15kg water barrelx4
  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~3:00 PM, Zone B -39%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 330km, water barrel x2
  - Cumulative fatigue: +30min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 35, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 26, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 14}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 118, 'duration_minutes': 47, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 400, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 25, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 34}}

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 317km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 13 units of relief supply box (53kg each) and 10 units of construction material (58kg each). The boat can travel at 23km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 19km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 32%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 37%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 242km waypoint, 5 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 104 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 74 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 37 minutes (1st: 74min, 2nd: 111min, 3rd: 148min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:
STEP0=meta · STEP1=given · STEP2=worked solution · STEP3=answer and verification

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 53kg relief supply boxx13 + 58kg construction materialx10
  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -37%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 242km, construction material x5
  - Cumulative fatigue: +37min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 24, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 19, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 16}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 104, 'duration_minutes': 74, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 11}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 16, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 32}}

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 333km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 17 units of water barrel (19kg each) and 7 units of medical kit (13kg each). The boat can travel at 21km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 45km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 36%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 280km waypoint, 6 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 104 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 62 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 27 minutes (1st: 62min, 2nd: 89min, 3rd: 116min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 7km/h (downstream in Zone A)
  - Cargo: 19kg water barrelx17 + 13kg medical kitx7
  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~1:00 PM, Zone B -36%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 280km, water barrel x6
  - Cumulative fatigue: +27min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 45, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 18, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 15}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 104, 'duration_minutes': 62, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 200, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 20, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 33}}

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 306km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 22 units of construction material (68kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (39kg each). The boat can travel at 24km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 25km is Zone A (speed limit: 19km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 81km waypoint, 3 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 108 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 65 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 28 minutes (1st: 65min, 2nd: 93min, 3rd: 121min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - River-trip simulation with cargo, congestion, and rests; final numeric answer lives only in [STEP 3].

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone A)
  - Cargo: 68kg construction materialx22 + 39kg relief supply boxx5
  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~1:00 PM, Zone B -34%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 81km, relief supply box x3
  - Cumulative fatigue: +28min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 25, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 19, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 14}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 108, 'duration_minutes': 65, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 9}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 400, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 16, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 34}}

question:
Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 333km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 22 units of water barrel (22kg each) and 7 units of construction material (74kg each). The boat can travel at 35km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 39km is Zone A (speed limit: 26km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 24% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 35%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 198km waypoint, 7 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 106 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 54 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 34 minutes (1st: 54min, 2nd: 88min, 3rd: 122min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -35%

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 319km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 11 units of medical kit (10kg each) and 5 units of construction material (59kg each). The boat can travel at 31km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 27km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 13km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 200km waypoint, 6 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 107 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 8km). The base rest duration is 46 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 37 minutes (1st: 46min, 2nd: 83min, 3rd: 120min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Riverside logistics driver Yoon is a veteran who has worked on this river for 5 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 304km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 14 units of construction material (69kg each) and 7 units of relief supply box (45kg each). The boat can travel at 28km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 198km waypoint, 8 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 100 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 74 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 29 minutes (1st: 74min, 2nd: 103min, 3rd: 132min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)

  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~3:00 PM, Zone B -33%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 198km, construction material x8
  - Cumulative fatigue: +29min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 20, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 21, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 14}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 100, 'duration_minutes': 74, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 9}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 18, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 35}}

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 307km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 22 units of construction material (65kg each) and 8 units of water barrel (17kg each). The boat can travel at 30km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 22km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 38%.

From 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 132km waypoint, 3 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 109 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 50 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 35 minutes (1st: 50min, 2nd: 85min, 3rd: 120min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~3:00 PM, Zone B -34%

  - Cumulative fatigue: +35min per rest

question:
River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 5 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 309km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 22 units of relief supply box (65kg each) and 6 units of medical kit (10kg each). The boat can travel at 28km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 37km is Zone A (speed limit: 19km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 37%.

From 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 154km waypoint, 4 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 109 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 84 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 38 minutes (1st: 84min, 2nd: 122min, 3rd: 160min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~2:00 PM, Zone B -33%

  - Cumulative fatigue: +38min per rest

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 5 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 338km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 22 units of medical kit (14kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (34kg each). The boat can travel at 22km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 32km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 13km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 36%.

From 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 216km waypoint, 14 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 97 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 8km). The base rest duration is 84 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 33 minutes (1st: 84min, 2nd: 117min, 3rd: 150min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~1:00 PM, Zone B -33%

  - Cumulative fatigue: +33min per rest

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 303km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 19 units of water barrel (15kg each) and 7 units of relief supply box (44kg each). The boat can travel at 28km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 32km is Zone A (speed limit: 22km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 19% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 209km waypoint, 11 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 95 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 74 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 35 minutes (1st: 74min, 2nd: 109min, 3rd: 144min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Marine operator Choi is a veteran who has worked on this river for 14 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 314km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 16 units of construction material (52kg each) and 10 units of relief supply box (66kg each). The boat can travel at 21km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 17km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 25% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 253km waypoint, 10 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 119 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 60 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 30 minutes (1st: 60min, 2nd: 90min, 3rd: 120min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:
STEP0=meta · STEP1=given · STEP2=worked solution · STEP3=answer and verification
[STEP 0] Problem meta
  - River-trip simulation with cargo, congestion, and rests; final numeric answer lives only in [STEP 3].
[STEP 1] Given (initial conditions & rules)
  - Total distance=314km, boat still-water speed=21km/h, departure=9:00 AM
  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone A)
  - Cargo: 52kg construction materialx16 + 66kg relief supply boxx10
  - Congestion: 11:00 AM~5:00 PM, Zone B -33%

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 322km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 19 units of relief supply box (58kg each) and 8 units of construction material (58kg each). The boat can travel at 33km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 45km is Zone A (speed limit: 27km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 34%.

From 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 38%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 190km waypoint, 5 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 108 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 74 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 36 minutes (1st: 74min, 2nd: 110min, 3rd: 146min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
River courier Seo is a veteran who has worked on this river for 6 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 312km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 14 units of relief supply box (35kg each) and 6 units of construction material (80kg each). The boat can travel at 26km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 39km is Zone A (speed limit: 18km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 35%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 180km waypoint, 5 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 119 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 57 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 38 minutes (1st: 57min, 2nd: 95min, 3rd: 133min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 35kg relief supply boxx14 + 80kg construction materialx6
  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~1:00 PM, Zone B -35%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 180km, relief supply box x5
  - Cumulative fatigue: +38min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 39, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 18, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 15}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 119, 'duration_minutes': 57, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 22, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 35}}

question:
Marine operator Choi is a veteran who has worked on this river for 14 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 372km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 13 units of construction material (72kg each) and 8 units of water barrel (16kg each). The boat can travel at 31km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 44km is Zone A (speed limit: 28km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 16km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 34%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 330km waypoint, 4 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 120 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 53 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 34 minutes (1st: 53min, 2nd: 87min, 3rd: 121min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 72kg construction materialx13 + 16kg water barrelx8
  - Congestion: 8:00 AM~2:00 PM, Zone B -34%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 330km, water barrel x4
  - Cumulative fatigue: +34min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 44, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 28, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 16}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 120, 'duration_minutes': 53, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 11}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 300, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 18, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 35}}

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 298km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 10 units of water barrel (18kg each) and 8 units of construction material (80kg each). The boat can travel at 35km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 29km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 33%.

From 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 40%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 240km waypoint, 5 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 120 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 60 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 33 minutes (1st: 60min, 2nd: 93min, 3rd: 126min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Current: 8km/h (downstream in Zone B)
  - Cargo: 18kg water barrelx10 + 80kg construction materialx8
  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~3:00 PM, Zone B -40%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 240km, construction material x5
  - Cumulative fatigue: +33min per rest
  - Regulations: {'speed_limit': {'zone_A_km': 23, 'zone_A_limit_kph': 29, 'zone_B_limit_kph': 14}, 'mandatory_rest': {'trigger_minutes': 120, 'duration_minutes': 60, 'rest_stop_interval_km': 10}, 'cargo_effect': {'heavy_load_kg': 200, 'zone_A_reduction_percent': 18, 'zone_B_reduction_percent': 33}}

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 362km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 21 units of medical kit (9kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (41kg each). The boat can travel at 22km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 35km is Zone A (speed limit: 19km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 15km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 24% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 35%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 33%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 280km waypoint, 3 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 103 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 76 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 36 minutes (1st: 76min, 2nd: 112min, 3rd: 148min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 344km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 15 units of water barrel (23kg each) and 7 units of relief supply box (69kg each). The boat can travel at 22km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 8km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 28km is Zone A (speed limit: 19km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 14km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 19% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 32%.

From 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 37%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 110km waypoint, 9 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 120 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 10km). The base rest duration is 76 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 31 minutes (1st: 76min, 2nd: 107min, 3rd: 138min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

  - Congestion: 9:00 AM~2:00 PM, Zone B -37%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 110km, water barrel x9
  - Cumulative fatigue: +31min per rest

question:
Riverside logistics driver Yoon is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 301km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 14 units of construction material (75kg each) and 5 units of water barrel (22kg each). The boat can travel at 28km/h in still water.

The river has a current of 7km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).

The first 27km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 13km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 37%.

From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 40%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

At the 144km waypoint, 2 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 114 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 77 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 27 minutes (1st: 77min, 2nd: 104min, 3rd: 131min, ...).

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
solution:

  - Congestion: 10:00 AM~4:00 PM, Zone B -40%
  - Mid-journey delivery: 144km, water barrel x2
  - Cumulative fatigue: +27min per rest
